body.title: [sprint] Prove AiderDevice on a REAL UU-package ticket end-to-end (clone-test isolation + large repo + push)
body.description:
**Problem:** T-aider-rack-device-wrapper proved the device live on a SELF-CONTAINED clone (real ticket -> aider qwen@Hex -> gate PASS -> close). Three surfaces remain unexercised, all real: (1) CLONE-TEST ISOLATION — `unseen_university` is an editable install using a MetaPathFinder (__editable__..._finder.py) that BEATS PYTHONPATH; empirically, tests run in a clone import the ORIGINAL working tree, not the clone's edits (would fake-green a UU-package ticket). Needs a fresh-venv-per-clone or finder-disabling test runner. (2) LARGE-REPO repo-map — the UU repo is 613 py files / 77MB; aider's repo-map orientation at that scale is untested (the sandboxes were tiny). (3) PUSH-TO-ORIGIN — the work branch must reach CC for validation (push the branch to the shared remote, guarded).

**Proposed shape:** Build the clone-test-isolation runner (fresh venv or PYTHONNOUSERSITE + finder disable); run aider on a real small failing/incomplete UU ticket against a full UU clone; push the branch; CC-validate.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/aider/runner.py (test-isolation + optional push); a proof harness under devlab/.
**Design rules:** never fake a green (editable-finder makes clone tests import original tree — MUST isolate); branch-not-main; relative paths (124553ee).
**Scope boundary:** the real-UU-ticket proof + isolation runner + push. NOT: merge-time proof (T-builder-merge-time-proof).
**Test plan:** the behavioral proof IS a real UU ticket going red->green through the device with correct (isolated) test resolution, branch pushed, CC-validated.
**Completion criteria:** >=1 real UU-package ticket built by the device end-to-end, tests green under correct import resolution, branch pushed, CC-validated.
**intention:** I intend that the aider builder is proven on the real UU repo, not just self-contained sandboxes, before it is trusted on the swarm.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
